reviews.review_msg: A very lovely instructor. She is totally caring, open-minded and very attentive to students. I usually have troubles during the first phase of the course, but she helped me to get through it easily.

Pros:
-detail notes (no need textbook)
-clearly explain 
-1 midterm only 
-midterm and final are quite easy if you go over her notes
Cons:
-quizzes are so much harder than midterm and final. it will double minus your score. however, you can read through the quizzes before the exam as she might use some of the concepts.
easy course

Kristin's style of teaching Economics is very good. I am not a good Eco student but taking it with her made me a bright Eco student. The biggest drawback of taking her is her quizzes which are of 75% of whole grade and inside those quizzes True False are negative marking. So if you cope up in the quizzes from the very beginning, Kristin is for you.

This is disaster, you literally studying nothing and then solving some random stuff, and when you are attending office hours, you will get answers like it is better to revise this chapter, as if i didnt read and revised it. Do not take her, seriously, the grade system is a disaster

A ridiculous grading system, attendance was optional. Have a quiz every single week and while aware that the average score was not well, did not have the effort of going through the quiz one more time and explain the work process. At the very least, she will answer your questions if you ask and does that quite clearly

Basically, taking her class is just listening to her lecture and taking notes, fortunately, she will give all the notes and all you need to do is fill in the blank. The course is curved so remember to knock your classmates out before the final exam^^. If you are at fic pick Kristin quick. She is the best you can get.
Do well in every quiz and assignment review your notes regularly it's really easy to achieve high marks in her class especially online courses.

Kristin is the best professor I've ever known. She is definitely my favourite. I would love to take more courses with her. Her explanations for the graphs are the best . To be honest, I used to hate doing calculation questions but she made them so easy to understand, I just fell in love with doing numericals. She's just awesome.

Super easy to follow teacher, her lectures consist of easy fill in the blank notes with tons of examples. Made the course easy to get an A and was very nice and helpful with any questions. Exams were primarily multiple choice with a few graphs. As long as you understand basic linear graphs, you'll be good. Definitely GPA booster. Would take again.

Comes off as a PTA mom. She's super nice and her grading criteria is clear. You'll benefit the most by going to tutorial and asking the TA for help. She incorporates 6 weird MC questions where you have to tick all the correct boxes to get the 1 mark. Average for the class was a C+ (common in 1year courses)

All she does is fill in the blanks notes and reads off her slides. Homework is for participation and the answers are posted online after for you to compare but she doesn't show how she got the answer. Tests are worth almost all your grade and she never gives you a review session. Teaches a 1st year clas as if you are a 3rd or 4th year econ student.

Although she is a very kind and respectable prof, her notes are very very easy compared to her tests (2 midterms and a final). You must make sure to do all the practice questions which are on Canvas but not mentioned in class. No need for a textbook as her notes are quite detailed. Make sure to come to class because it is a theory-heavy course.
She's a very quirky person, lectures themselves are really boring, but she gives great notes so you have to attend to get them down.

When I came to her office hours for help she sighed at me being there, was short with me, and told me to just read the notes and question. I read all the notes and questions several times and was asking so I can understand it better because her notes weren't good. She was annoyed that I was there and didn't give me clarity of anything I asked about

Kristin comes off as a really nice person during lectures but her expectations and grading scheme are far from nice. She gave a final exam worth 50% where the class average ended up being 54% and she refused to scale the class. The class average grade ended up being a C. She also loves 6 part MC and highly detailed graph questions. AVOID HER!!!
definitely a gpa booster.

She is a really nice person but better off as a counsellor than a teacher, tries her best but rather not engaging at all in lectures,makes mistakes while teaching which might misguide you for the midterms. Do not take this course for a GPA booster, she will grade you very strict and get ready for graphs.

I absolutely loved her notes. they were fill in the blanks which were easy to follow and made us pay attention in class. (i usually hate copying long notes from lectures... but fill in the blanks were more engaging imo). Her exams were actually pretty easy if you follow her notes (the examples are in the notes are similar to her exams).

Lecture was 3 hours of listening to her read directly off the slides which were fill in the blank and she doesn't post them after so you have to attend every lecture just to write a few words. At the end of every class you get to waste an hour watching her do a participation quiz that you copy and hand in for attendance.
One of the best professor in econ103, clear teaching and easy to understand. Everything you need is from her textbook and nothing else.

I think she is the best professor. She will answer every student's question. She care about her student. She is also very pretty. I am very proud to study with this professor. I will definitely take her course again.
she is perferct
Gpa booster confirmed. Studied only  2 days for each test and got an A
Kristin gives clear notes and explanation. She is nice and helpful. Do not have any quizzes only midterm and final. Exams are not too hard to get a pass.

She is a sweetheart! She is always super available, and replies e-mails relatively quickly. Her class is structured nicely. There is a lot of material to cover, so beware. But if you keep up with the notes & assignments, you should be well-prepared. Her MC are definitely tricky, but written is straight-forward. A great semester!
Great teacher
Amazing professor, just need to redo all the assignment and read the notes, you'll for sure pass it. Also, she's really nice, if I can, I'll definitely choose her lecture again.
I got a C in her class, but I still think she is an excellent professor!I like her teaching style.
Honestly an amazing prof. Incredibly nice, and smart. There's no reason you shouldn't get it least an A- in this class, not 1 complaint about her
It's an easy course to ace if you're literate in maths and graphs. Study her notes and redo her assignments and snatch that A+!
Nice prof, so sweet and helpful, perfect
Course itself, probably easier amongst the 200 level ones. Otherwise, she's really nice, approachable and wears a lot of green for an environmental econ class(:
She's good
useless textbook.boring lecture!(just copy your friend's notes)exams are straight off from the notes!lots to memorize. she's nice though
Kristin is a good teacher, she helps student a lot. Her lecture very straightforward from the textbook.

About 95% of the exam questions are straight from her notes. Just memorize them and you'll definitely get a good mark. This class is a GPA booster, but you probably won't learn anything new if that's what you're hoping to do.

Overall her exams are fair. But she tends to focus on what I think are unnecessary details. For example, she asks questions like what the author argues on page XX. And what are the arguments in a particular article. We have to know them out of the blue and I barely realize that's going to improve our economic skill. But overall she is good.

wow, i got a way higher mark in micro/macro economics than this course, even though this course has no econ prerequsites. shes makes such a simple course extreemly difficult, with boring long notes and articles. shes acyually discouraged me from studying econ. By far worst econ prof there is.
just plain awful. don't know how she's a prof, she cant teach at all. so much you need to know for exmas that are pretty hard. this is supposed to be a first year econ course. NOT A GPA BOOSTER

Cannot teach at all. Don't even bother to buy a book; her lectures are directly from the book, and all you do during the class is writing her notes, which she writes in class using an overhead. Class is extremelly borring; so if you are interested in Econ, do not take it with her. Her exams are not hard, but her marking system is totally unfair.

First of all she's not hot AT ALL, second shes goes way too fast when teaching that no one knows what is going on. her exams are brutal and the tutorials require you to make a couple presentations that are marked extrremly hard by the dull TA's. this is supposed to be an easy class so plz do yrself a favor and do not take it with her.

Totally unorganized, showed no creativity while teaching, examples are dry, uninteresting and often dragged on for too long. she gives you no breaks during lectures. Her marking scheme is inflexible and unforgiving, you are pretty much deprived of all creativity and are expected to memorize her examples and hers only. We did nothing in tutorial.
She is nice and hot, her final is not too hard, but her F range is so big (2 times of A range), and the avg. dont very high (58% in MT) watch out!!!!
The course is too easy. And she is so hot! Never feel bored in class.
Great prof, very organized. Easy assignment and her notes are all you need. If you study her notes, you can do good in midterm and final. Highly recommend.

Nice teacher. This course is really hard. The class average for the midterm was 58%. She scaled the the class so everyone can do well. Her notes are original. She is clear in lectures and helpful during office hours. Must work hard on assignments and final is everything.   Give the full three hours for the final.
a fairly easy course...easy assignments, midterms and finals...just look at the notes and ur be finee...da text sux...dont even need to buy it! A easy marker...and she looks pritti gdd..!!
easy class. mt and final were fair. nice prof. notes were organized and easy to understand. recommended.

Just finished the final today. She didn't give us ANY MT questions neither in midterm nor in final. But all written questions on both exams were EASY if you actually studied HER NOTE. DONT EVEN BOTHER TO BUY THE TEXT IT WAS THE WORST TEXT I EVER SEEN! Thanks to Kristin, her notes are very clear, easy to follow, and also easy to understand.
Easy class. just read her notes, dont even bother buying the textbook cuz u r NOT gonna read it. Her practice final exam questions are the same as those REAL FINAL exam questions ^_^...SWEET!!

seems like her first time teaching so shes pretty unorganized. emails the lecture notes to the class late the night before lecture. assignments are emailed to the class pretty late too. a class website would be VERY helpful to get the lecture notes and assignments. very clear and is teaching a really easy subject luckily. and quite good looking.
No Comments
No Comments
